title: Bananas Are Slightly Radioactive

scenes.text: Every banana you eat is very slightly radioactive.
They're packed with potassium, and a tiny fraction of all potassium is a radioactive isotope called potassium forty.
It's so tiny that scientists half-jokingly measure radiation in banana equivalent doses.
You'd have to eat millions in one sitting to be in any danger, so eat up.
scenes.label: COMPLETELY SAFE

scenes.text: Follow for a mind-blowing fact every day.

description: Bananas are naturally, very slightly radioactive - thanks to potassium-40. Scientists even measure radiation in 'banana doses'. Totally safe. Follow for daily facts!
